title: malaxis palustris
intro: Malaxis palustris, also known as the marsh adder's mouth, is a type of orchid that is commonly found in wetland areas. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The health benefits of Malaxis palustris include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Malaxis palustris can be made by drying the plant, then infusing it in hot water or oil to create teas, tinctures, or salves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
